title: **LiveBench: A Challenging, Contamination-Limited LLM Benchmark**
model_or_system: Multiple models (0.5B–405B); GPT-4o, Claude, Llama, Qwen, etc.
reported_results: Top models achieve <70% accuracy across 6 categories (reasoning, math, coding, language, data analysis, instruction following)
setup_notes: Official LLM benchmark; monthly-updated questions from arXiv, news, competitions, IMDb; 1,436 total questions across 18 tasks; automatic objective grading

title: **Certainty Robustness: Evaluating LLM stability under self-challenging prompts**
reported_results: Two-turn evaluation on 200 LiveBench reasoning/math questions; models vary in resistance to conversational pressure
setup_notes: Uses LiveBench (reasoning & math subsets) for certainty robustness evaluation; evaluates how models change answers when challenged

title: **CodeAlignBench: Assessing Code Generation Models on Developer-Preferred Code Adjustments**
reported_results: Models show varying performance across instruction-following dimensions; translated LiveBench coding tasks into Java/JavaScript
setup_notes: Uses LiveBench programming tasks (code_completion, code_generation); multi-language evaluation; tests constraint adherence and refinement ability

title: **INFERENCEDYNAMICS: Efficient Routing Across LLMs through Structured Capability and Knowledge Profiling**
reported_results: Demonstrated superior routing efficiency and accuracy on LiveBench vs. baseline approaches; strong performance on MMLU-Pro, GPQA, BigGenBench
setup_notes: LLM routing framework; tested on LiveBench alongside MMLU-Pro, GPQA, BigGenBench; evaluates capability modeling across diverse benchmarks

title: **RLAnything: Forge Environment, Policy, and Reward Model in Completely Dynamic RL System**
reported_results: 18.7% gain on AlfWorld; 11.9% improvement on LiveBench
setup_notes: Reinforcement learning framework for LLMs & agentic systems; integrates environment, policy, reward models with closed-loop optimization

title: **HybridFlow: Resource-Adaptive Subtask Routing for Efficient Edge-Cloud LLM Inference**
reported_results: Maintains competitive accuracy on LiveBench-Reasoning while reducing latency and API cost significantly vs. baselines
setup_notes: Edge-cloud inference framework; evaluates on GPQA, MMLU-Pro, AIME24, and LiveBench-Reasoning; dependency-aware DAG routing with learned utility models

title: **dLLM-Serve: Taming the Memory Footprint Crisis in Diffusion LLM Serving**
reported_results: 1.61×–1.81× throughput improvement on consumer GPU; 1.60×–1.74× on server GPU; tail latency reduced by ~4×
setup_notes: Production serving system for parallel-decoding LLMs; tested on LiveBench, Burst, and OSC workloads; addresses memory footprint during inference

title: **SABER: Switchable and Balanced Training for Efficient LLM Reasoning**
reported_results: LiveBench-Reasoning: SABER-FastThink cuts reasoning length 65.4% with 3.6% accuracy gain vs. base; evaluated on MATH, GSM8K, MBPP; graceful degradation across inference modes
setup_notes: Reinforcement learning for token-budgeted reasoning; supports 4 discrete inference modes (NoThink, FastThink, CoreThink, DeepThink); length-aware rewards

title: **Thought Manipulation: External Thought Can Be Efficient for Large Reasoning Models**
reported_results: 30% token reduction on LiveBench/Code while maintaining performance; reduced output token counts with minimal CoT generator overhead
setup_notes: Optimization technique for large reasoning models; manipulates internal chain-of-thought generation; tests on LiveBench coding dataset

title: **TICKing All the Boxes: Generated Checklists Improve LLM Evaluation and Generation**
reported_results: STICK self-refinement on LiveBench reasoning: +7.8% absolute gain; Best-of-N selection: +6.3% on WildBench
setup_notes: Automated evaluation protocol using LLM-generated instruction-specific checklists; improves generation quality via self-refinement; structured multi-faceted evaluation

title: **LMMs-Eval: Reality Check on the Evaluation of Large Multimodal Models** (includes Multimodal LiveBench)
reported_results: Comprehensive evaluation across 50+ tasks; Multimodal LiveBench uses continuously-updated news/forums for zero-contamination assessment
setup_notes: Unified multimodal benchmark framework; introduces Multimodal LiveBench with continuously updating news & online forums; low-cost, zero-contamination approach

title: **Generalising LLM Routing using Past Performance Retrieval: A Few-Shot Router is Sufficient** (ContextualRouter)
reported_results: Performance matches or exceeds complex routers in quality-cost trade-offs across SPROUT, RouterBench, LiveBench, BigGenBench, EmbedLLM
setup_notes: Few-shot routing framework; retrieval-based approach; k-NN averaging baseline; tested on 5 routing benchmarks including LiveBench

title: **Teach2Eval: An Interaction-Driven LLMs Evaluation Method via Teaching Effectiveness**
reported_results: Spearman correlation with LiveBench: 0.975; vs. Chatbot Arena: 0.944; outperforms direct evaluation; fine-grained ability decomposition (Application/Judgment/Guidance/Reflection)
setup_notes: Reframes evaluation as teaching: candidate model guides weaker students; student gains measure capability; evaluated on 60 datasets; robust to contamination & saturation

title: **LiveWorld: Simulating Out-of-Sight Dynamics in Generative Video World Models**
model_or_system: Video world model (Zicheng Duan et al.)
reported_results: Proposed dedicated LiveBench for video task; enables out-of-sight scene dynamics persistence
setup_notes: Introduces LiveBench for video generation benchmarking; distinct from text/MM LiveBench; evaluates temporal coherence and entity state tracking

title: Think you have Solved Question Answering? Try ARC, the AI2 Reasoning Challenge
setup_notes: Original benchmark paper introducing ARC; 7,787 natural grade school science questions; largest public-domain QA dataset

title: KG^2: Learning to Reason Science Exam Questions with Contextual Knowledge Graph Embeddings
setup_notes: Uses contextual knowledge graphs for reasoning on science questions

title: A Systematic Classification of Knowledge, Reasoning, and Context within the ARC Dataset
setup_notes: Annotates ARC with knowledge and reasoning type labels; establishes taxonomy

title: Quick and (not so) Dirty: Unsupervised Selection of Justification Sentences for Multi-hop Question Answering
setup_notes: Unsupervised selection of relevant sentences for ARC

title: FreeLB: Enhanced Adversarial Training for Natural Language Understanding
setup_notes: Adversarial training at embedding level; state-of-the-art single-model results at publication

title: Finetuned Language Models Are Zero-Shot Learners (FLAN)
setup_notes: Instruction tuning on 60 NLP tasks; strong generalization to unseen tasks

title: Think you have Solved Direct-Answer Question Answering? Try ARC-DA
reported_results: ARC-Challenge: 81.4% / ARC-Easy: 92.7% (on MC converted from DA format)
setup_notes: Direct-answer version of ARC; 2985 questions with multiple valid answers; evaluation metrics for open-response format

title: UNIFIEDQA: Crossing Format Boundaries with a Single QA System
setup_notes: Unified system trained on 4 QA formats; generalizes well across dataset formats

title: UnifiedQA-v2: Stronger Generalization via Broader Cross-Format Training
setup_notes: Extended training on ~3x more QA datasets than UnifiedQA

title: General-Purpose Question-Answering with Macaw
reported_results: Evaluates on subset of ARC questions
setup_notes: Multi-angle approach for diverse QA formats; samples equally from 7 QA datasets

title: The ConceptARC Benchmark: Evaluating Understanding and Generalization in the ARC Domain
model_or_system: Human baseline vs GPT-4, ARC-Kaggle winners
reported_results: Humans substantially outperform all machine solvers
setup_notes: Systematic evaluation of concept learning; tests abstraction and generalization abilities

title: LLMs and the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus: Successes, Failures, and the Importance of Object-Based Representations
reported_results: Mixed results on ARC; LLMs struggle with visual reasoning
setup_notes: Analysis of LLM performance on ARC tasks; discusses object representation importance

title: H-ARC: A Robust Estimate of Human Performance on the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus Benchmark
reported_results: ARC human performance estimates with ranges
setup_notes: Establishes human baseline metrics; discusses methodological issues in human evaluation

title: Sci-CoT: Leveraging Large Language Models for Enhanced Knowledge Distillation in Small Models for Scientific QA
setup_notes: Knowledge distillation from LLMs to small models; reports strong results on ARC datasets

title: T5-ARC: Test-Time Training for Transductive Transformer Models in ARC-AGI Challenge
setup_notes: Novel test-time training approach for improving performance on ARC-AGI

title: ARC Prize 2024: Technical Report
setup_notes: Competition results; discusses frontier AGI reasoning techniques, program synthesis, test-time training

title: ARC 'Challenge' Is Not That Challenging
reported_results: Challenge accuracy 64% → 93% with fairer eval methods
setup_notes: Critical analysis showing evaluation setup affects perceived difficulty; suggests LLM dataset artifacts

title: AugARC: Augmented Abstraction and Reasoning Benchmark for Large Language Models
reported_results: Results comparing original ARC vs augmented version
setup_notes: Augmented benchmark designed for comprehensive LLM evaluation

title: MADIL: An MDL-based Framework for Efficient Program Synthesis in the ARC Benchmark
reported_results: Shows LLMs recently improved ARC performance
setup_notes: Minimum Description Length approach for ARC program synthesis

title: A Comparative Study: Reasoning with Small T5-Based Models on Abstract Reasoning Corpus (ARC)
reported_results: Comparative analysis of small T5 variants
setup_notes: Studies pattern learning and reasoning in SLMs; reports results across T5 model sizes

title: ARC-AGI-2 Technical Report
setup_notes: New ARC-AGI-2 benchmark introduced; discusses evolution of evaluation

title: Playgrounds for Abstraction and Reasoning
reported_results: Human vs AI performance on Mini-ARC
setup_notes: Introduces smaller ARC variant; six categories including commonsense tasks

title: ARC-AGI without pretraining
setup_notes: Explores solving ARC without large language models

title: LLMs and Abstract Reasoning: Successes, Failures, and Object-Based Representation
reported_results: 20% accuracy with GPT-3 on converted text
setup_notes: Converting visual ARC to text for LLMs; analysis of representation issues

title: Do Large Language Models Solve ARC Visual Analogies Like People Do?
setup_notes: Analysis of how LLMs approach visual reasoning on ARC vs human strategies

title: Understanding LLMs' Fluid Intelligence Deficiency: An Analysis of the ARC Task
reported_results: High accuracy on text-based tasks; challenges for visual LLMs
setup_notes: Detailed analysis of LLM performance on ARC; fluid intelligence vs learned knowledge

title: Challenge Closed-Book Science Exam: A Meta-Learning Based Question Answering System
setup_notes: Meta-learning approach without external knowledge bases

title: Attending Knowledge Facts with BERT-like Models in Question-Answering
setup_notes: Studies integration of knowledge with BERT for QA; examines why external facts help

title: The Surprising Effectiveness of Test-Time Training for Few-Shot Learning
setup_notes: Explores task-specific knowledge leverage for few-shot scenarios including ARC

title: In Benchmarks We Trust... Or Not?
model_or_system: Benchmark analysis including ARC, SuperGLUE, WinoGrande
setup_notes: Critical examination of widely-used benchmarks including ARC; discusses evaluation concerns

title: Shortcutted Commonsense: Data Spuriousness in Deep Learning of Commonsense Reasoning
model_or_system: Analysis of commonsense datasets including ARC
setup_notes: Examines spurious patterns and shortcuts in commonsense reasoning benchmarks

title: A Context-Aware Neural Network for the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC)
setup_notes: Novel neural network design for context-aware reasoning on ARC

title: Benchmarking Machine Reading Comprehension: A Psychological Perspective
setup_notes: Evaluates MRC benchmarks including ARC from cognitive science perspective

title: Optimizing Large Language Models: A Deep Dive into Effective Prompt Engineering Techniques
reported_results: Results on ARC dataset with multiple prompt strategies
setup_notes: Comprehensive analysis of prompt engineering techniques on ARC and other datasets

title: A Survey of Prompt Engineering Methods in Large Language Models for Different NLP Tasks
setup_notes: Systematic survey of prompting techniques; shows prompt engineering impact on LLM performance

title: Pencil Puzzle Bench: A Benchmark for Multi-Step Verifiable Reasoning
model_or_system: GPT-5.2, Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet, Llama models, OpenAI, Anthropic
reported_results: GPT-5.2: 81x improvement with reasoning effort; Claude Opus 4.6: 0.3% to 30.0% with iterative verification; GPT-5.2 max effort: 20.2% to 56.0%
setup_notes: 300 puzzles across 20 varieties from 62,231 total; direct ask (single-shot) vs agentic (multi-turn with verification); per-move reward signals; densely verifiable intermediate states

title: MultiZebraLogic: A Multilingual Logical Reasoning Benchmark
reported_results: o3-mini 4x5 puzzles: baseline accuracy reported; 5 red herrings decrease accuracy by 15±7%; language/theme independence demonstrated
setup_notes: 128+1024 puzzles per size; 2x3 and 4x5 grid sizes; 14 clue types, 8 red herring types; 9 Germanic languages; controllable difficulty

title: RiddleBench: A New Generative Reasoning Benchmark for LLMs
model_or_system: Gemini 2.5 Pro, o3, Claude 4 Sonnet, state-of-the-art models
reported_results: Gemini 2.5 Pro: 60.30%, o3: 63.37%, Claude 4 Sonnet: 63.16%; all struggle with constraint satisfaction and spatial reasoning
setup_notes: 1,737 challenging puzzles in English; integrates logical deduction, spatial awareness, constraint satisfaction; reveals hallucination cascades and poor self-correction

title: Enigmata: Scaling Logical Reasoning in Large Language Models with Synthetic Verifiable Puzzles
reported_results: Qwen2.5-32B-Enigmata surpasses o3-mini-high and o1; ARC-AGI: 32.8%, ARC-AGI 2: 0.6%; AIME 2024-2025 improvements; GPQA Diamond gains
setup_notes: 36 tasks across 7 categories; unlimited generator with controllable difficulty; rule-based verifier; multi-task RLVR training compatible

title: SATBench: Benchmarking LLMs' Logical Reasoning via Automated Puzzle Generation from SAT Formulas
reported_results: o4-mini: 65% on hard UNSAT, near 50% random baseline; systematic failures in satisfiability bias, context inconsistency
setup_notes: 2100 puzzles with automated generation and validation; LLM-based + solver-based consistency checks; human validation subset; adjustable difficulty

title: Sudoku-Bench: Evaluating creative reasoning with Sudoku variants
reported_results: Less than 15% of SOTA LLMs solve puzzles unaided; baseline evaluation on diverse variants
setup_notes: Curated challenging unconventional Sudoku variants; standardized text-based representation; flexible tools for thousands of public puzzles; tests novel logical breakthroughs

title: LogicPuzzleRL: Cultivating Robust Mathematical Reasoning in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
model_or_system: LLMs trained via RL with verifiable rewards
reported_results: RL training improves out-of-distribution performance on math benchmarks; significant gains on mid-difficulty multi-step problems
setup_notes: 7 custom logic puzzles: constraint propagation, spatial consistency, symbolic deduction; binary feedback based on correctness; improves algebraic manipulation, geometric inference, combinatorial logic

title: AutoLogi: Automated Generation of Logic Puzzles for Evaluating Reasoning Abilities of Large Language Models
model_or_system: 8 modern LLMs (Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4, Gemini, Llama, etc.)
reported_results: Performance range 35-73% (vs 21-37% on multiple-choice sources); better model differentiation with open-ended format
setup_notes: Bilingual benchmark; program-based verification; controllable difficulty; rejection sampling for training data generation

title: Logic.py: Bridging the Gap between LLMs and Constraint Solvers
reported_results: 65% absolute improvement: baseline to 90%+ accuracy on ZebraLogicBench via LLM formalization + constraint solver
setup_notes: Domain-specific language (logic.py) for constraint specification; combines LLM strengths with symbolic solver capabilities; state-of-the-art on logic puzzles

title: Evaluating Implicit Biases in LLM Reasoning through Logic Grid Puzzles
reported_results: Consistent accuracy improvements when solutions align with stereotypes; systematic bias in deductive reasoning demonstrated
setup_notes: PRIME framework: stereotypical, anti-stereotypical, neutral puzzle variants; automatic generation and verification; variable complexity and biased settings

title: HardcoreLogic: Challenging Large Reasoning Models with Long-tail Logic Puzzle Games
model_or_system: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) including o1, GPT-5 variants
reported_results: Top benchmark performers show significant performance drops on non-canonical variants; heavy reliance on memorized stereotypes exposed
setup_notes: 5000+ puzzles across 10 games; 3 transformation dimensions (Increased Complexity, Uncommon Elements, Unsolvable Puzzles); tests robustness and rule adaptation

title: Liar, Liar, Logical Mire: A Benchmark for Suppositional Reasoning in Large Language Models
reported_results: Models struggle with truth/lie inference; lower-performing models exhibit diverse reasoning errors; proficient models struggle with false statement implications
setup_notes: TruthQuest benchmark based on Knights & Knaves; varying complexity (character count, statement types); tests hypothetical scenario reasoning

title: LogicVista: Multimodal LLM Logical Reasoning Benchmark in Visual Contexts
model_or_system: 8 mainstream MLLMs (GPT-4V, Claude, Gemini Vision, Llava, etc.)
reported_results: Comprehensive evaluation across 448 multiple-choice questions; identifies proficiency gaps in visual logical reasoning
setup_notes: 5 logical reasoning tasks with 9 different capabilities; visual context required; annotations with correct answers and human-written reasoning; open-ended + multiple-choice evaluation

title: Frontier LLMs Still Struggle with Simple Reasoning Tasks
model_or_system: Frontier LLMs including thinking models (GPT-o4, Gemini, Claude, OpenAI o-series)
reported_results: Even state-of-the-art thinking models consistently fail on trivial variants; systematic failures in memorization vs. logic separation
setup_notes: Procedurally generated simple reasoning tasks (counting, first-order logic, proof trees, travel planning); unpuzzles dataset with trivialized known puzzles; variable parameters for computational complexity

title: Oedipus and the Sphinx: Benchmarking and Improving Visual Language Models for Complex Graphic Reasoning
model_or_system: 11 mainstream VLMs (GPT-4V, Claude Vision, Gemini, LLaVA, etc.)
reported_results: VLMs show deficiencies in complex graphic reasoning; 33.5% performance improvement with DiaCoT + ReasonTune
setup_notes: ReasonBench: 1,613 questions from real-world intelligence tests; location, attribute, quantity, multi-element tasks; spatial, relational, abstract reasoning evaluation
